title: Knapsack Problem

content.problemStatement:
You have a knapsack that can take upto WmaxW_{max}Wmax​ weight.

Given NNN items, ithi^{th}ith of them having the weight WiW_iWi​ and value ViV_iVi​. 

You can pick some or all the items completely so that their weight does not exceed WmaxW_{max}Wmax​.

Your task is to maximise the total value of the items you will carry in your bag.


Input Format

The first line contains two integer NNN and WmaxW_{max}Wmax​, denoting the total number of items and capacity of the knapsack
Next NNN lines contains the description of the items.

ithi^{th}ith line contains two integers WiW_iWi​ and ViV_iVi​, denoting the Weight and Value of the ithi^{th}ith item




Output Format
Output the maximum value you can carry while not exceeding the weight limit of the knapsack.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with two integers N and Wmax — the number of items and the maximum weight the knapsack can hold. Then N lines follow, each containing two integers Wi and Vi — the weight and value of the i‑th item.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum total value achievable without exceeding the weight limit.

solution.approach: The problem is the classic 0/1 knapsack where each item can be taken at most once. Because the capacity Wmax is at most 10^4, a one‑dimensional DP over weight is optimal. dp[c] stores the maximum value achievable with total weight exactly c (or at most c). For each item we iterate capacities from Wmax down to the item's weight and try to improve dp[cap] with dp[cap‑Wi] + Vi. The answer for a test case is dp[Wmax]. This runs in O(N·Wmax) time and O(Wmax) memory per test case, easily fitting the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * Wmax) per test case, O(T * N * Wmax) overall.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0 <= i <= 20)
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 0:
        print(1)
        print('1 1')
        print('1 1')
    elif i == 1:
        print(1)
        print('1 5')
        print('6 10')
